2605.00348.title: Block-wise Codeword Embedding for Reliable Multi-bit Text Watermarking
2605.00348.paper_summary: Recent multi-bit watermarking methods for large language models (LLMs) prioritize capacity over reliability, often conflating decoding with detection. Our analysis reveals that existing ECC-based extractors suffer from catastrophic false positive rates (FPR), and applying rejection thresholds merely collapses detection sensitivity (TPR) to random guessing. To resolve this structural limitation, we propose \textbf{BREW} (Block-wise Reliable Embedding for Watermarking), a framework shifting the paradigm to \emph{designated verification}. BREW employs a two-stage mechanism: (i) \textbf{blind message estimation} via independent block voting, followed by (ii) \textbf{window-shifting verification} that rigorously validates the payload against local edits. Experiments demonstrate that BREW achieves a TPR of 0.965 with an FPR of 0.02 under 10\% synonym substitution, demonstrating that the high-FPR issue is not an inherent trade-off of multi-bit watermarking, but a solvable structural flaw of prior decoding-centric designs. Our framework is model-agnostic and theoretically grounded, providing a scalable solution for reliable forensic deployment.
2605.00348.paper_summary_zh: 最近的大型語言模型（LLMs）多位元水印方法優先考慮容量而非可靠性，經常將解碼與檢測混為一談。<lb/>我們的分析顯示，現有的基於ECC的提取器存在災難性的假陽性率（FPR），而應用拒絕閾值僅僅使檢測靈敏度（TPR）降至隨機猜測。<lb/>為了解決這一結構性限制，我們提出了\textbf{BREW}（區塊可靠嵌入水印），這是一個將範式轉向\emph{指定驗證}的框架。<lb/>BREW採用兩階段機制：（i）通過獨立區塊投票進行\textbf{盲消息估計}，隨後是（ii）\textbf{窗口移動驗證}，該驗證嚴格地將有效載荷與局部編輯進行比對。<lb/>實驗表明，BREW在10\%同義詞替換下達到0.965的TPR和0.02的FPR，證明高FPR問題並不是多位元水印的固有權衡，而是先前以解碼為中心的設計的一個可解決的結構缺陷。<lb/>我們的框架是模型無關的，並且在理論上是有根據的，為可靠的取證部署提供了一個可擴展的解決方案。
2605.00348.author: Joeun Kim et.al.
2605.00348.authors: Joeun Kim, HoEun Kim, Dongsup Jin, Young-Sik Kim
